segments.text: No single adjustment has saved us more money than the pink line rule. We've all been there
We make mistakes and promise ourselves we won't make them again
We tell ourselves stuff like okay, I'll just stop selling early and see no improvements to P&L
Well, duh. We aren't coming up with a solution to the problem
We're here to tell you that there is one mistake that is more subtle and more expensive than all the other ones combined
And you can fix it today
The pink line rule is the cheat code that tells us exactly when to stay on the sidelines and when to strike
But here's the catch the rule is an absolute
There are very specific moments where it's okay to break the rule in fact, it's encouraged
I'm excited to talk about this because it's been a game changer in my own trading
Welcome to the trading floor podcast episode 8
I'm Tim Belden here with me is Garrett, Dr. and and we have our guy Kurt behind the scenes making the magic happen
So Garrett when did you start thinking about this rule?
Well, I mean I automatically have a story for you. So let's do it. We were trading Tesla and
Tesla had developed a range. I can't remember the price level. I can't remember when this was
But it was in a
Pretty tight range and it was setting up for a breakout and we were watching this thing every day because it was gonna be a good breakout
Every day off the open it would run
And it would run in a way that would like stand out from all the other stocks of clearly exhibiting relative strength
Getting your attention. I mean you look at the tape and it was like doing that
Tesla thing and it just it looked like it was gonna go forever and
Every single day I'm thinking in my mind like it's happening now
Because it looked like if this thing just keeps going it's going to break out and that's gonna be it, right? So
every single morning I was
Chasing this thing off the open and then it would just stuff it would just it would it wouldn't even get to the breakout level
And it would just completely collapse and go all the way back to the lows sometimes take the lows out
And then hold somewhere and gather itself like by then I was
Stopped out I'd already lost money, but

Until finally it broke out and

like I just burned like so much of my P&L

But at the same time like I've seen so many experienced traders

For experienced traders and also just to kind of take a look at the whole methodology behind this pink line rule
